rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or declared as helpers in the same annex.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings instead.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are properly declared or visible in the current scope.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for constants and equation declarations.
Validate that expression types match expected contract types (e.g., bool for comparisons).
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation contracts in implementation scopes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scope should reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Helper declarations (eq, const) must be in the same annex as their usage and reference only visible identifiers.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by adding new architecture elements; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings.
Do not introduce package-qualified names unless they are already present in the model.
